title: Tune autovacuum for a hot table
description: Adjust per-table autovacuum settings and monitor bloat so dead tuples are reclaimed before they hurt.
body: Steps: 1. Measure dead tuples and last vacuum via pg_stat_user_tables and bloat estimates. 2. If a high-churn table accumulates dead tuples faster than the global default reclaims, lower its autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor and threshold per-table so it triggers sooner. 3. Watch for long-running transactions or replication slots holding back the xmin horizon (vacuum cannot remove tuples newer than the oldest transaction). 4. Run a manual VACUUM (ANALYZE) if stats are stale; reserve VACUUM FULL for a maintenance window since it takes an exclusive lock. Rollback point: revert per-table settings. Validation: dead tuple count stabilizes and index/seq scan plans stop degrading.
